---
title: "Exploring Storms"
author: "ishika-ravat"
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
```

## Named Storms Per Year

Below is a stacked area chart showing the count of each storm type per year (tropical depressions, tropical storms, and hurricanes) in the `dplyr::storms` dataset.

```{r plot-storms}
storms %>%
  filter(status %in% c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane")) %>%
  mutate(status = factor(status, levels = c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane"))) %>%
  distinct(name, year, status) %>%
  count(year, status) %>%
  complete(year, status, fill = list(n = 0)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = n, fill = status)) +
  geom_area(position = "stack", alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "tropical depression" = "#93C5FD",
      "tropical storm" = "#F97316",
      "hurricane" = "#B91C1C"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "tropical depression" = "Tropical Depression",
      "tropical storm" = "Tropical Storm",
      "hurricane" = "Hurricane"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Storm Types Per Year",
    subtitle = "Based on the dplyr::storms dataset",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Storms",
    fill = "Storm Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```
